rename-and-remove-tracked-files.label: Rename and Remove Tracked Files
rename-and-remove-tracked-files.summary: Maintain tracked community-site paths safely and record one structural cleanup.

sketch0.title: Tracked Paths Change with the Project

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Rename the Tracked Page
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt: Type `mkdir -p pages` and press Enter. Then type `git mv index.html pages/events.html` and press Enter.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Use Git to move the tracked path into pages/.

help.concept: git mv updates the working tree and stages the tracked rename.
help.hint_2: Do not copy the file and leave the old path behind.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.solutionCode:
mkdir -p pages
git mv index.html pages/events.html

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer'

' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer'

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.instructions: Type `mkdir -p pages` and press Enter. Then type `git mv index.html pages/events.html` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

1.message: Did you run `git mv index.html pages/events.html`?

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Remove the Obsolete Note
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt: The page rename is already staged. Type `git rm notes/old-events.txt` and press Enter. Then type `git diff --staged --name-status` and press Enter.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Remove the tracked obsolete note through Git, then inspect the structural summary.

help.concept: git rm removes a tracked path from the working tree and stages that removal.
help.hint_2: The summary should show a rename and a deletion.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -- pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -- pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.instructions: The page rename is already staged. Type `git rm notes/old-events.txt` and press Enter. Then type `git diff --staged --name-status` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.title: Commit the Structural Cleanup
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.prompt: The rename and removal are staged. Type `git commit -m "Organize community files"` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.hint: Record the related structural changes in one focused commit.

help.concept: Related path maintenance can form one commit when it serves one cleanup purpose.
help.hint_2: The short status should be empty afterward.

try_rename_and_remove_tracked_files_sket.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Organize community files"
git status --short

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html rm -f -- community-site/notes/old-events.txt rmdir -- community-site/notes 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Update community heading' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Space community footer' rm -f -- community-site/index.html rm -f -- community-site/notes/old-events.txt rmdir -- community-site/notes 2>/dev/null || true mkdir -p -- community-site/pages printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/pages/events.html git -C community-site add -A

single-choice-1.title: Purpose of git mv
single-choice-1.prompt: What is the primary purpose of the `git mv` command?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To move a file without tracking
options.b: To rename a file and stage the change

single-choice-2.title: Effect of git rm
single-choice-2.prompt: What happens when you use the `git rm` command?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is moved to a new location
options.b: The file is removed from the working directory and staged for deletion

multi-choice-1.title: Commands to Verify Staged Changes
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which commands can you use to verify staged changes before committing?
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

drag-reorder-1.title: Steps to Rename and Commit a File
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the steps to rename a file and commit the change using Git.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Use Git-aware structural operations, review the staged paths, and record a cleanup without losing earlier versions.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t1: Use git mv to rename the file
tokens.t2: Check the status with git status
tokens.t3: Commit the change with git commit

sketch0.title: Tracked Paths Change with the Project
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: Projects reorganize. `git mv` stages a tracked move, and `git rm` stages a tracked removal. The important result is the before-and-after snapshot, not a special rename object stored forever.

sketch1.title: Structural Changes Need Review
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: `git diff --staged --name-status` summarizes staged additions, deletions, and likely renames. Review the path change before recording it.

sketch3.title: Group One Cleanup Purpose
sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Moving the community page and removing its obsolete note serve one organization purpose, so they can share one focused commit.
